title: lobelia neglecta
intro: Lobelia neglecta, also known as Indian tobacco or blue lobelia, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States and parts of Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the nervous system and act as a sedative. Lobelia neglecta is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues, and is sometimes used as a treatment for asthma and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Lobelia neglecta include infusions, tinctures, and smoke, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
